Charles Mouhot (1828 — 1904) was a younger brother of Henri Alexandre Mouhot, the famous French explorer who gave a vivid description of Angkor in 1859. His Journal and letters, later collated in the book Voyage dans les Royaumes de Siam, de Cambodge et de Laos(1868) for the French public, became a sensation for his description of Angkor, which he reached from the South in January 1860 (chapters 18, 19, 20).
